channel.title: CGT News Podcast
channel.description: Your daily automated digest of cell and gene therapy news, covering CAR-T, gene editing, CRISPR, viral vectors, and more.
channel.ns0:author: CGTPod Bot

item.title: CGT Daily News - April 20, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - UCB plots epilepsy expansion with $650M Neurona cell therapy acquisition - Three gene therapy pioneers just won the Breakthrough Prize. This is their story - UCB pays $650M+ for Neurona, marking 'strategic expansion' into regenerative medicine - How WuXi AppTec’s Integrated Platform Is Helping Therapies Move Faster from Discovery to Patients

item.title: CGT Daily News - April 17, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - Roche to start a new Phase 3 trial for Elevidys - MeiraGTx buys back gene therapy from J&J; Storm brings in $56M Series C - MSF says Gilead's lenacapavir supply 'not nearly enough'; PolyPeptide’s strategic review - Johnson & Johnson hands back failed eye disease gene therapy to MeiraGTx - Eying 3 approvals in 2 years, Ocugen’s CEO wants to bring gene therapy to the masses

item.title: CGT Daily News - April 16, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - In vivo autoimmune CAR-T race grows as two RNA startups enter the clinic - FDA official confirms plausible mechanism principles not exclusive to bespoke gene therapies - Replimune looks ahead as repeat CRL speeds the company's decline - Revolution Medicines leads latest wave of stock offerings, totaling $1.5B - Parker Institute doubles down on cancer vaccines as part of ongoing reboot - Cell therapy biotech Obsidian leverages Galera reverse merger to go public - Astellas to close stem cell unit’s Seattle site, with layoffs incoming - FDA narrows in on search for new biologics and vaccines leader - Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: Astellas shutters cell therapy site; Replimune reduces staff

item.title: CGT Daily News - April 13, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - FDA rejects Replimune cancer therapy, saying company didn't resolve trial doubts - China biotech’s CRISPR therapy suggests US drugmakers will face competition - Vivatides gets $54M; Wegovy drops cold chain in EU; Gilead takes Kymera option - FDA rejects Replimune’s melanoma prospect for second time - China-based CAR-T company Oricell raises $40M more as it looks to go public

item.title: CGT Weekly Digest - Week 15, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - In a first, a CAR-T cell therapy drives 3 autoimmune diseases into remission in a single patient - China’s Oricell raises $110M for carcinoma CAR-T plans ahead of IPO push

item.title: CGT Daily News - April 09, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - David Sinclair startup Life Biosciences raises $80M for clinical test of anti-aging gene therapy - Astellas unearths Dyno muscle disorder AAV capsid with $15M option agreement - Biogen links up with Alloy platform to boost ASO drug development

item.title: CGT Weekly Digest - Week 14, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - Aspect Biosystems gets $79M from Canadian government for diabetes cell therapy

item.title: CGT Daily News - April 02, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - Making custom CRISPR therapies could be harder than initially thought - FDA delays decision on Orca Bio cell therapy after filing more manufacturing data - FDA extends review of Orca Bio’s novel cell therapy for blood cancers

item.title: CGT Daily News - April 01, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - Astellas doesn’t pick up AviadoBio’s gene therapy, but nonprofit funding will move it forward - PepGen’s muscle disease program posts poor mid-stage results as one patient's data get markedly worse - First clinical trial of tRNA therapy will start soon

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 30, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - FDA approves Rocket's gene therapy for ultra-rare immune disease - AstraZeneca’s in vivo CAR-T led to early responses, but one death in China trial - AstraZeneca’s in vivo CAR-T bet eradicates cancer in 3 of 5 patients, but death mars dataset

item.title: CGT Weekly Digest - Week 13, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - Innate ends work on NK cell engager in phase 1/2 trial as part of ongoing pipeline refocus

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 26, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - After tumultuous year, Sarepta plots rebound with early data on RNA therapies - Beam looks to accelerated approval for AATD base editing after promising update - Sarepta's big bet on Arrowhead's siRNA assets yields early data

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 25, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - Ocugen heads to Phase 3 with gene therapy for geographic atrophy - Q&A: Controversial embryo editing entrepreneur Cathy Tie on her new startup - Ocugen eye disease gene therapy hits ph. 2 targets but underwhelms investors - Former Acorda CEO Ron Cohen emerges at Parkinson’s cell therapy biotech Oryon - Sanofi extends $180M to California T cell engager startup Kali - Sanofi returns to T-cell engager territory with $1.2B pact for phase 1-stage trispecific - Oryon shines out of stealth with $21M to fight Parkinson's disease

item.title: CGT Weekly Digest - Week 12, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - Briggs Morrison's Crossbow unveils $77M Series B for T cell engagers - Shanghai biotech Excalipoint gains $68.7M for T cell engagers - Crossbow hits $77M series B round to bankroll quiver of T-Bolts

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 20, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - CSL says its hemophilia B gene therapy is out of stock - AstraZeneca deepens UK-China ties with cell therapy R&D hub, manufacturing plans - AstraZeneca to build cell therapy manufacturing hub, R&D center in Shanghai

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 19, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - FDA opens door to RMAT designations for therapies on clinical hold - Beyond seizures: With experimental drug, Stoke aims to restore developmental loss in Dravet syndrome - Ionis delays prion disease readout; Arvinas' Parkinson's biomarker data - Aspen's personalized Parkinson's therapy shows early promise - Aspen's 1-year data for Parkinson's cell therapy plant seeds for phase 3 trial

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 18, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - China mRNA therapy startup Innorna to test gout treatment in US - Sana sees 14-month insulin production for transplanted islet therapy in single Type 1 diabetes patient - Inovio trims team as trouble brews at FDA for rare disease candidate

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 17, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - China startup CirCode gets clearance for trial of circular RNA therapy - Astellas walks away from $1.6B biobucks T-cell engager pact with CytomX after 6 years - Ultragenyx’s gene therapy hits key endpoint in Phase 3 trial for blood ammonia disease

item.title: CGT Daily News - March 13, 2026
item.description: Today's CGT news highlights: - A simpler form of DNA may be key to non-viral gene therapy, study suggests - Ultragenyx gene therapy hits co-primary endpoint in phase 3 rare disease study, teeing up potential win - Regenxbio posts clean safety profile for DMD gene therapy as pivotal data loom - Study suggests long non-coding RNA has potential as new class of genetic medicine - How a new tool for large-scale gene editing could power novel genetic medicines - China is going after the world's most expensive drugs: Endpoints Signal
